items.title: Construct skill missing scripts/state.py — skill broken on deploy
items.description: The construct skill generator produces SKILL.md that references `scripts/state.py` in every workflow phase (assess-save, assess-load, propose-save, propose-load, build-save, build-load, deploy-save, review, config). But state.py is not generated. Any agent that receives the construct skill and runs a construct cycle will hit 'file not found' on the first command. The production Clawdius deployment has this script at openclaw/workspace/skills/construct/scripts/state.py — it's a 300-line Python3 argparse CLI that manages construct state as JSON in the memory directory.
steps: Read the production state.py from home-server/openclaw/workspace/skills/construct/scripts/state.py to understand the interface
Add a generateStatePy() function to src/workspace/skills/construct.ts that returns the script content
The script should be generic (no Clawdius-specific paths) — use ~/.openclaw/ relative paths via Path.home()
Add 'scripts/state.py' to the return value of generateConstructSkill()
Ensure the writer sets chmod +x on the script (verify writer.ts handles nested skill scripts)
Add a test that verifies construct skill output includes scripts/state.py
acceptanceCriteria: generateConstructSkill() returns a 'scripts/state.py' key with valid Python3 content
The generated state.py handles all commands referenced in SKILL.md: assess-save, assess-load, propose-save, propose-load, build-save, build-load, deploy-save, review, config
The script runs without errors when invoked with --help

items.title: Template system fractured — three parallel sources of truth cause divergence
items.description:
There are three template systems that have diverged:

1. src/init/templates.ts — 6 templates as TypeScript objects, used by the init wizard. These drive the full generator (tools, identity, skills, Dockerfile, cron).
2. configs/templates/*.yaml — 6 identical templates as YAML, richer (has model_routing_strategy, day_in_the_life, use_case_mapping, skill_bundle.recommended). Used by src/templates/loader.ts.
3. src/templates/mapper.ts — its own MapperAnswers type and bundle generator that duplicates init/generate.ts but produces INCOMPLETE bundles (dockerfile: '', workspaceTools: {}, skills: {}).

Any feature touching templates (integration management, provider management, skill bundling) has to choose which system to use. The mapper path silently produces broken bundles. The YAML templates have valuable fields the code templates lack. This must be unified before more features build on top.
steps: Audit the three template systems: document every field in code templates vs YAML templates vs mapper types
Design unified TemplateChoice type that covers all fields from both sources (code + YAML)
Update configs/templates/*.yaml to be the canonical source — add any fields only in code templates
Update src/templates/loader.ts to produce TemplateChoice objects compatible with the init wizard
Replace src/init/templates.ts hardcoded array with a function that loads from configs/templates/*.yaml via the loader
Update src/templates/mapper.ts to delegate bundle generation to src/init/generate.ts instead of its own parallel generator
Remove all parallel generation logic from mapper.ts
Update mapper tests to verify full bundle output (dockerfile, workspaceTools, skills are populated)
Verify: clawhq init --guided, clawhq template preview, and the mapper path all produce identical bundle structure
acceptanceCriteria: configs/templates/*.yaml is the single source of truth for all template data
src/init/templates.ts loads from YAML, not hardcoded TypeScript objects
mapper.ts delegates to init/generate.ts — no parallel generation code
All three entry points produce complete DeploymentBundle with populated dockerfile, workspaceTools, and skills
YAML template fields (model_routing_strategy, day_in_the_life, use_case_mapping) are preserved and accessible
All existing tests pass after unification
No net increase in template-related code — consolidation not expansion
